title: abutilon glaucum
intro: Abutilon glaucum, also known as Indian mallow, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae, native to tropical Africa and Asia, and is used as a medicinal herb. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Abutilon glaucum include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional medicine to make ointments and salves for topical application.
